channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Darcy Breen Signs New Two-Year Deal with ACT Brumbies
item.description: Darcy Breen, the 27-year-old prop, has secured a two-year Super Rugby contract with the ACT Brumbies, keeping him in Canberra until the end of 2028. Arriving initially on a train-and-trial deal last N...

item.title: Lara Flynn Boyle Spotted in Los Angeles After Nearly a Year Away from Public Eye
item.description: Lara Flynn Boyle, the Emmy-nominated actress renowned for her roles in Twin Peaks and The Practice, was seen in Los Angeles on Sunday, marking her first public appearance in almost a year. The 56-year...

item.title: Shooting at San Diego Mosque Leaves Five Dead, Including Two Teens
item.description: On Monday, a tragic incident occurred at the Islamic Centre of San Diego, California, where two teenage gunmen opened fire, resulting in the deaths of a security guard and two other men outside the mo...

item.title: 1939 BMW 328 Roadster: Iconic Pre-War Sports Car and Racing Champion
item.description: The 1939 BMW 328 Roadster is widely celebrated as one of the most important and successful sports cars of the pre-World War II era. Renowned for its lightweight construction and aerodynamic design, it...

item.title: APC senatorial primaries produce upsets, disqualifications, and internal disputes
item.description: For real now, the APC senatorial primaries wey happen all over Naija don show say politics no dey joke. Some sitting senators like Ned Nwoko from Delta North, Neda Imasuen from Edo South, and Jibrin I...

item.title: Jose Mari Chan Prepares for Major Christmas Concert
item.description: Filipino music icon Jose Mari Chan is getting ready for a highly anticipated concert, signaling that the Christmas season is approaching even earlier this year. Known for his timeless Christmas songs ...

item.title: Trump delays planned Iran attack but remains ready for large-scale action
item.description: On Monday, President Donald Trump announced that he would postpone a planned attack on Iran that was scheduled for Tuesday. Despite the delay, Trump emphasized on Truth Social that he has instructed h...

item.title: Empowering Marginalised Girls in Kenya’s ASAL Regions Through Entrepreneurial Training
item.description: For many years, girls in Kenya’s arid and semi-arid lands (ASAL) were largely seen only in the context of marriage, with some married off as early as eleven, often in exchange for livestock. This prac...

item.title: San Francisco hosts a $10,000 treasure hunt within city limits
item.description: San Francisco is once again the stage for an exciting treasure hunt, with organisers hiding a $10,000 chest somewhere within seven miles of City Hall, buried just one foot underground. The event, laun...

item.title: Investigation into Teen Attack at San Diego Islamic Center Leaves Five Dead
item.description: On Monday morning in San Diego, two teenagers carried out a shooting at the Islamic Center of San Diego, killing three people before taking their own lives. Authorities identified one of the suspects,...

item.title: Homeless man arrested for stealing over £1,200 worth of designer perfume in City of London
item.description: A 46-year-old homeless man named Paul Michael Whitehead was arrested by City of London Police after stealing designer perfume worth more than £1,200 from a shop in the Square Mile. Store staff recogni...

item.title: The Evolution and Artistry of Late Victorian Wallpaper
item.description: During the late Victorian era, wallpaper became a central decorative element in middle-class homes, reflecting a period of rich visual and artistic expression. Moving away from the simpler and sometim...

item.title: Joburg Faces Potential Power Disconnections Amid R5.2bn Debt to Eskom
item.description: Eskom has warned the City of Johannesburg that it may disconnect electricity supply to various areas, including Sandton, Soweto, Fourways, Orange Farm, and Ivory Park, if a R5.2 billion debt is not se...

item.title: NIST physicists measure gravitational constant after decade-long experiment, finding small discrepancy
item.description: For over two centuries, determining the exact value of the universal gravitational constant, known as big G, has challenged scientists. Stephan Schlamminger and his team at the National Institute of S...

item.title: MEA Defends India's Human Rights and Media Record Amid Norwegian Press Queries
item.description: During Prime Minister Narendra Modi's recent visit to Norway, questions arose regarding his decision not to take queries from journalists during a joint press statement with the Norwegian Prime Minist...

item.title: PNP to Charge Individuals Spreading Violence Online After Senate Shooting
item.description: MANILA, Philippines — The Philippine National Police (PNP) has announced that five individuals spreading false information and inciting violence on social media following the recent shooting at the Se...

item.title: Ombudsman requests media list related to May 13 Senate shooting coverage
item.description: The Office of the Ombudsman has formally requested a list of media personnel who covered the gunfire incident in the Senate on May 13, 2026. The move aims to support the ongoing investigation into the...

item.title: Pep Guardiola Set To Step Down After Decade At Manchester City
item.description: Pep Guardiola, wey don dey manage Manchester City since 2016, fit step down after dis season. The Spaniard don win plenty trophies for City, including six Premier League titles and the club's first-ev...

item.title: CDF Field Marshal Asim Munir Stresses Preparedness Against Terrorism and Modern Warfare During Quetta Visit
item.description: Chief of Army Staff and Chief of Defence Forces Field Marshal Asim Munir has stated that externally sponsored terrorism, along with propaganda and disinformation campaigns, cannot stop Pakistan's prog...

item.title: Karua Faces Criticism Over Narrow Comparison of Moi and Ruto Eras Amid Rising Fuel Prices
item.description: Former Justice Minister Martha Karua has sparked debate online after comparing fuel prices during President Daniel arap Moi's tenure in June 1997 with current fuel costs under President William Ruto. ...
